intro: Silene principis, also known as the Prince's Catchfly, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Silene principis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
